claims.proof: tests/test_ai_native.py and tests/test_command_contract.py exercise CLI, direct-call, MCP, and llms.txt projections from registered commands.
claims.notes: The same definition now feeds local stdio and the embeddable modern ASGI HTTP binding; hosted deployment recipes remain tracked separately.

claims.proof: src/milo/verify.py defines the ten checks; tests/test_verify.py covers success, failure, discovery, MCP Apps, gateway, and subprocess transport outcomes.
claims.notes: FastMCP inspect exposes server metadata and protocol views; the comparison does not mislabel inspection as absence of verification capability.

claims.proof: .github/workflows/ci.yml runs the suite on CPython 3.14t with PYTHON_GIL=0 on Linux and macOS, plus a GIL-on compatibility lane.
claims.notes: This proves Milo's runtime lanes, not the pending hosted parallel-tool-call comparison.

claims.proof: pyproject.toml declares only kida-templates in project.dependencies; uv.lock is the lock receipt.
claims.notes: Development, documentation, and benchmark dependency groups are outside the runtime claim.

claims.proof: benchmarks/BASELINE.md records workload, platform, Python version, medians, and explicit post-baseline spot-check context.
claims.notes: These are Milo-local workload snapshots, not comparative Typer or FastMCP speed claims.

claims.proof: tests/unit/test_mcp_http.py proves two handlers overlap in one CPython 3.14t process with PYTHON_GIL=0. No hosted throughput claim is authorized until pounce #229 produces an artifact-backed cross-server chart.
claims.notes: Repository-level parallel execution is proven; hosted throughput remains pending and must not be promoted before pounce #229.
